Title: Overview
Component: Ensuring the Government and the Public Benefit from a Competitive American AI Marketplace
Theme: Ensuring a Competitive American AI Marketplace

Title: Overview
Component: Safeguarding Taxpayer Dollars by Tracking AI Performance and Managing Risk

Title: Overview
Component: Promoting Effective AI Acquisition with Cross-Functional Engagement

Title: Update Agency Policies
Component: Update internal procedures on acquisition to comply with M-25-22 and M-25-21.

Title: Maximize the Use of American-Made AI
Component: Maximize the use of AI products and services that are developed and produced in the United States.
Theme: Ensuring a Competitive American AI Marketplace

Title: Protect Privacy
Component: Establish policies and processes to ensure compliance with privacy requirements.

Title: Protect IP Rights and Use of Government Data
Component: Have appropriate processes for addressing use of government data and include appropriate contractual terms.

Title: Scoping licensing and other IP rights
Component: Scope licensing and other IP rights appropriately to avoid vendor lock-in.
Theme: Ensuring a Competitive American AI Marketplace

Title: Timeline
Component: Ensure components necessary to operate and monitor the AI system remain available.

Title: Data Handling
Component: Provide clear guidance on handling, access, and use of agency data.

Title: Use of Government Data
Component: Ensure contracts permanently prohibit the use of non-public inputted agency data and outputted results to further train publicly or commercially available AI algorithms without explicit agency consent.

Title: Documentation, Transparency, and Accessibility
Component: Prioritize obtaining documentation that facilitates transparency and explainability.

Title: Spotlight AI Acquisition Authorities, Approaches, and Vehicles
Component: GSA to develop a plan to release publicly available guide(s) for the acquisition workforce.

Title: Contribute to a Shared Repository of Best Practices
Component: GSA to develop a web-based repository for sharing information, knowledge, and resources about AI acquisition.

Title: Determine Necessary Disclosures of AI Use in the Fulfillment of a Government Contract
Component: Determine whether there are circumstances that merit including a provision in a solicitation requiring disclosure of AI use.

Title: Convening a Cross-Functional Team
Component: Convene an internal cross-functional team to inform the procurement of AI systems or services.

Title: Determining the Use of High-Impact AI
Component: Make an initial determination of whether a system is likely to host high-impact AI use cases.

Title: Broad Market Research
Component: Conduct thorough market research to seek state-of-the-art AI capabilities.
Theme: Ensuring a Competitive American AI Marketplace

Title: Product Demonstration
Component: Seek detailed demonstrations and tests of potentially useful AI systems or services.

Title: Performance-Based Acquisition Techniques
Component: Use performance-based techniques to identify requirements and contract terms.

Title: AI Use Transparency Requirements
Component: Disclose in solicitations whether a planned use of an AI system meets the threshold of a high-impact use case.

Title: Protections Against Vendor Lock-In
Component: Include provisions in the solicitation reflecting the agency's interest in AI proposals that reduce the risk of vendor lock-in.
Theme: Ensuring a Competitive American AI Marketplace

Title: IP Rights and Use of Government Data
Component: Include appropriate terms related to IP rights and lawful use of government data.

Title: Testing and Evaluation
Component: Test proposed solutions to understand the capabilities and limitations of any offered AI system or service.

Title: Opportunity and Risk Re-Evaluation
Component: Assess proposals for potential new AI-related risks that were not previously identified.

Title: IP Rights and Use of Government Data
Component: Include contract terms consistent with the processes adopted by the agency per Section 3(d).

Title: Privacy
Component: Include contract terms that address privacy considerations as described in Section 3(c).

Title: Vendor Lock-In Protections
Component: Include contract terms to reduce the risk that switching vendors could become cost-prohibitive.
Theme: Ensuring a Competitive American AI Marketplace

Title: OMB Memorandum M-25-21 Compliance Requirements
Component: Contracts must ensure compliance with minimum risk management practices for high-impact use cases as required under M-25-21.

Title: Ongoing Testing and Monitoring
Component: Contractual terms must provide the contracting agency the ability to regularly monitor and evaluate performance, risks, and effectiveness of an AI system or service.

Title: Vendor Performance Requirements
Component: Require vendors to regularly monitor an AI system's performance and rectify unacceptable behavior.

Title: New Feature Notification
Component: Require vendors to provide a notification to relevant agency stakeholders prior to the integration of new AI enhancements, features, or components.

Title: Authorization To Operate Compliance
Component: Any AI systems and services operated as an information system by or on behalf of an agency must receive an authorization to operate.

Title: Contractual Oversight
Component: Perform effective system oversight consistent with the terms of the contract.

Title: Performance and Cost Justification
Component: Arrange for periodic evaluation of the AI system or service's value to the government.

Title: Sunset Criteria
Component: Determine criteria for sunsetting the use of an AI system.

Title: Vendor Lock-In Protection
Component: Implement any contractual terms related to ongoing rights and access to any data or derived products resulting from the services provided under the contract.
Theme: Ensuring a Competitive American AI Marketplace
